title: Wendlandia spicata
taxonomy: Wendlandia spicata is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of flowering plants that are widely distributed across the globe. The genus Wendlandia itself is a part of the tribe Rubieae, a group of plants characterized by their distinctive flowers and fruit. Wendlandia spicata is specifically placed within the genus due to its shared morphological and anatomical traits with other species within the genus. The classification of Wendlandia spicata serves as a means of organizing and understanding its relationships with other plant species.
uses: Wendlandia spicata, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been found to possess medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
